headlineSummary: HN sentiment skews negative for Microsoft Copilot and OpenAI, with a close tie driven by limited evidence, while Google Gemini and Anthropic are more mixed with a close tie [E1][E3].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot draws clearly negative discussion, with one comment describing incorrect implementation choices and saying there is "so much going wrong here"; it is in a close tie with OpenAI on limited evidence [E3].

OpenAI also gets clearly negative feedback, as a comment says ChatGPT Codex and other models "basically fail or try to shortcut nearly every task," and it is in a close tie with Microsoft Copilot on limited evidence [E1].

Google Gemini is viewed more neutrally overall, but one comparison says the workflow works "a lot better" with Claude than with Gemini, so the evidence stays limited and it is close to Anthropic [E2].

Anthropic lands in mixed-neutral territory: one comment praises Claude as working "a lot better" for the workflow, while another says Claude Code models "basically fail or try to shortcut nearly every task," leaving a close tie with Google Gemini [E1][E2].

evidence.excerpt: As a real world example, I was told to evaluate Claude Code and ChatGPT codex at my current job since my boss had heard about them and wanted to know what it would mean for our operations. Our main environment is a C# and Typescript monorepo with 2 products being developed, and even with a pretty extensive test suite and a nearly 100 line "AGENTS.md" file, all models I tried basically fail or try to shortcut nearly every task I give it, even when using "plan mode" to give it time to come up with a plan before starting.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude Code and says the models tried 'basically fail or try to shortcut nearly every task,' which is strong criticism of Anthropic's coding model quality.
The comment explicitly names ChatGPT codex and applies the same strong criticism that the models tried 'basically fail or try to shortcut nearly every task.'

evidence.excerpt: That said, I've found that this kind of workflow works a lot better with claude than with gemini.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly compares Claude favorably, saying the workflow works 'a lot better' with it.
In the direct comparison, Gemini is presented as performing worse than Claude for this workflow.

evidence.excerpt: The copilot agent then implemented that by adding a replace statement, which is not how you are supposed to do this. It also included some seemingly-unrelated changes. The copilot reviewer called out the unrelated changes, but the human maintainer apparently didn't notice and merged anyway. There is just so much going wrong here.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly refers to 'The copilot agent' and describes incorrect changes and 'so much going wrong here,' which is strong negative assessment of Copilot's output quality.
